title: carthamodes hispanica
intro: Carthamodes hispanica, also known as Spanish thistle, is a thistle-like plant native to the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe. It is a biennial herb with medicinal properties attributed to its leaves, flowers, and roots, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant is known for its anti-inflammatory, diuretic, and expectorant properties, making it beneficial for conditions such as bronchitis, fever, and digestive problems, while also providing relief from pain and swelling. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as dried and used in topical applications, showcasing its versatility in traditional medicine.
